title: DepthVision3D: A versatile deep learning dataset for 3D vision
abstract: Deep learning-based 3D reconstruction from a single or a few images has become an important area of research with significant engineering applications. However, its research progress has been constrained by the lack of high-quality training datasets. In this paper, we have prepared a new high-accuracy versatile dataset designed for such deep learning-based 3D reconstruction and depth estimation methods. The dataset provides rich images and labels for a variety of supervised and unsupervised learning. To show the usage of the dataset, we also present a Hierarchical Depth Network (HiDNet), which is a generalized image-to-depth regression network implemented in PyTorch. HiDNet employs a U-Net architecture with attention mechanisms to capture salient features, serving as a baseline for active single-shot structured-light settings. By prioritizing reproducibility and clarity over architectural novelty, it provides an effective foundation for future research. The open benchmark includes a dataset, code, and evaluation protocol for consistent evaluation.
